raw_response:
Tool Call: retrieve("tooth anatomy and cusps")

Retrieved Documents: 32 documents

Based on the gathered evidence regarding tooth anatomy and cusps, particularly concerning the mandibular second molar, there isn't any indication suggesting it would consistently possess five cusps. Most descriptions align with the typical configuration of the mandibular second molar featuring fewer cusps—typically just two on each buccal and lingual sides—as opposed to the four observed in its predecessor, the mandibular first molar. While variations do exist across individuals, general consensus among studies shows that five-cusped configurations are far rarer in mandibular second molars compared to others listed. Therefore, maintaining our stance consistent with prior discussions, my agreement is reaffirmed:
\[ \boxed{A} \]
This consistency reinforces the correctness of selecting the mandibular second molar as distinctly different in cusp count pattern amongst given choices.
